metadata.summary: Extract multi-step business logic into Service Objects with canonical ServiceResult. Wrap writes in transactions, move side effects outside, prevent N+1 with Bullet.

content.title: Rails Patterns (Service Objects and ServiceResult)
content.summary: Rails 7.x applications extract multi-step business logic into Service Objects that accept params and current_user in initialize and return a single canonical ServiceResult from #call. Wrap multi-step writes in ActiveRecord::Base.transaction. Move mailers, webhooks, and HTTP calls to after_commit callbacks or a transactional outbox — never mid-transaction. One ServiceResult class for the entire codebase.

why.p: Fat controllers and fat models with callbacks lead to unpredictable execution order and test coupling. ServiceResult gives controllers a uniform success/failure contract without exception sniffing. The most dangerous agent-generated bug is calling .deliver_later or HTTP inside a transaction: a rolled-back insert leaves a sent email or a charged card.
Bullet in test mode with raise: true catches N+1 at CI time. A single canonical ServiceResult ensures every service returns the same shape, making controllers predictable and testable.

list.item: Rails 7.x app where controllers or ActiveRecord callbacks have grown unwieldy.
Multi-step writes that must be transactional plus side effects (mailers, audit logs, webhooks).
Defining a uniform success/failure return shape so controllers do not rescue exceptions.
Refactoring fat controllers and models toward service-centric architecture.
Tiny single-step writes (User.create!(params)) — wrapping in a service is ceremony.
Read-only endpoints — services add no value over a query object or scope.
Background jobs that already encapsulate one action — nesting Service inside Sidekiq inside Service is over-engineering.
Apps using Trailblazer, Interactor, or Dry::Transaction — stick to one paradigm.

section.name: canonical-service-result
section.p: Define exactly one ServiceResult at app/services/service_result.rb. All service objects return it from #call. CI must fail if a second definition is found. Use success?/failure? as the only API.

rule.statement: Define exactly one ServiceResult at app/services/service_result.rb. All service objects return it from #call. CI must fail if a second definition is found. Use success?/failure? as the only API — never ok?, result?, or error?.
rule.rationale: LLMs invent slightly different ServiceResult shapes per service file (sometimes result.ok?, sometimes result.success?, sometimes result.failure?). A divergent API means every controller must handle multiple shapes. Lock the API in CI with a simple grep.

example.title: ServiceResult Class
Controller Usage Pattern
example.code:
module Api module V1 class UsersController < ApplicationController def create result = Users::CreateService.new( params: user_params, current_user: current_user ).call
if result.success? render json: UserSerializer.new(result.data), status: :created else render json: { errors: result.errors }, status: :unprocessable_entity end end
private
def user_params params.require(:user).permit(:name, :email, :password) end end end end

section.name: service-object-structure
section.p: Service objects accept params and current_user in initialize. The public API is #call returning ServiceResult. All private helpers are private. Namespace by domain: Users::CreateService.

rule.statement: Service objects accept params: and current_user: in initialize. The public API is #call returning ServiceResult. All private helpers are private. Namespace by domain: Users::CreateService.
rule.rationale: A consistent interface means every service is discoverable and can be tested the same way. Namespacing by domain avoids collisions and makes the service directory navigable.

rule.statement: Move mailers, webhook HTTP calls, and search index updates to after_commit callbacks, an outbox, or chained Sidekiq jobs. Never call deliver_later or external HTTP inside ActiveRecord::Base.transaction.
rule.rationale: deliver_later inside a transaction enqueues the job before the transaction commits. If the transaction rolls back, the worker runs against a non-existent record. The race window is small but real under load.

rule.statement: Controllers call params.require(...).permit(...) before passing to the service. Services receive an already-permitted hash. Services must not call params directly.
rule.rationale: Strong Parameters enforce an allowlist at the HTTP boundary. Services receiving a raw params object bypass the allowlist, enabling mass assignment attacks on any field the service writes.

example.title: Service Object with Transaction
example.code:
# app/services/users/create_service.rb module Users class CreateService def initialize(params:, current_user: nil) @params = params @current_user = current_user end
def call user = User.new(user_params)
ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do user.save! create_audit_log(user) end
# Side effects AFTER commit UserMailer.welcome(user).deliver_later ServiceResult.success(user) rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e ServiceResult.failure(e.record.errors.full_messages) end
private
def user_params = @params.slice(:name, :email, :password)
def create_audit_log(user) AuditLog.create!(action: 'user.created', resource: user, actor: @current_user) end end end

section.name: antipatterns-and-gotchas
section.p: Common failure modes in LLM-generated Rails service code: divergent ServiceResult, non-idempotent jobs, N+1 in serializers, and authorization gaps.

antipattern.description: Multiple files defining ServiceResult with different method names (ok?, success?, result?, error?).
antipattern.reason: Controllers must handle every variant. Add a CI step that greps for class ServiceResult and fails if more than one definition exists. Lock to success?/failure? only.

antipattern.description: Sidekiq jobs that accept ActiveRecord objects directly: perform(user) instead of perform(user.id).
antipattern.reason: ActiveRecord objects serialise as YAML and load a potentially stale record when the worker runs. Pass IDs; load fresh records inside perform.

antipattern.description: Sidekiq jobs without idempotency — double-charging, double-sending on retry.
antipattern.reason: Workers retry on failure. Idempotency requires checking a unique key before performing the side effect. Use a database flag or SidekiqUniqueJobs.

antipattern.description: N+1 queries in ActiveModel::Serializer or Jbuilder templates, not caught by controller specs.
antipattern.reason: Serialisers run after the controller action. Bullet must be enabled in test mode with raise: true and serialiser specs must be run, not just controller specs.

rule.statement: ApplicationController must call verify_authorized and verify_policy_scoped (Pundit). Every controller action must call authorize @resource or policy_scope(Resource) explicitly.
rule.rationale: Pundit does not enforce authorization automatically. Without verify_authorized, an agent adding a new action may forget to call authorize, leaving it unprotected.

rule.statement: Never generate drop_column or change_column migrations without a backfill plan. Require reversible migrations — every change_table block must have a reversible counterpart.
rule.rationale: Irreversible migrations cannot be rolled back. Agents generate destructive migrations routinely without checking if the column has data or if the rollback is needed during a deploy.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Use SDD: spec → migration → model + factory → service object + RSpec service spec → controller + request spec → serializer. Reviewer agent must enforce one ServiceResult shape across the codebase, ban side effects inside transactions (use after_commit or Outbox.enqueue), and run bundle exec rubocop + bundle exec rspec per task. Bullet should run in test mode and fail tests on N+1.

rule.statement: One canonical ServiceResult (app/services/service_result.rb); all services return it. CI fails on duplicates.
rule.rationale: Prevents API divergence across the codebase.

rule.statement: Side effects (mailers, webhooks, search-index updates) live in after_commit callbacks, an outbox, or chained Sidekiq jobs — never mid-transaction.
Mailers and outbound HTTP calls go through .deliver_later / perform_async so the request thread stays fast.
rule.rationale: Synchronous external calls block request completion.

rule.statement: Use Pundit policies; controllers call authorize @resource on every action and policy_scope on collection endpoints.
rule.rationale: Explicit authorization prevents agent-generated authorization gaps.

rule.statement: Strong Parameters in controllers; services receive an already-permitted hash.
Keep ActiveRecord callbacks for invariants only (before_validation :normalize_email); business logic stays in services.
rule.rationale: Callbacks are invisible and hard to test when they contain business logic.

rule.statement: Run Bullet, Brakeman, and bundler-audit in CI; gate merges on green.
rule.rationale: Catches N+1, security issues, and CVEs at CI time.

section.name: ai-agent-gotchas
section.p: Common pitfalls when LLM agents generate Service Object code.

antipattern.description: LLMs invent slightly different ServiceResult shapes per file (sometimes result.ok?, sometimes result.success?, sometimes result.failure?). Lock the API in CI.
antipattern.reason: Controllers must handle every variant. Add a CI step that greps for class ServiceResult and fails if more than one definition exists.

antipattern.description: Agents put UserMailer.welcome(user).deliver_now between save! and end of transaction — a rolled-back insert leaves a sent email. Always deliver_later after commit.
antipattern.reason: deliver_now within a transaction runs before commit completes.

antipattern.description: N+1 patterns reappear in serializers (ActiveModel::Serializer/Jbuilder); reviewer must check serializer specs, not just controller specs.
antipattern.reason: Serializers run after the controller action, outside the spec's SQL spy.

antipattern.description: Sidekiq jobs generated by AI often accept ActiveRecord objects directly — pass IDs, not objects, to avoid serialization of stale state.
antipattern.reason: AR objects serialize as YAML and load stale records when the worker runs.

antipattern.description: Agents skip Pundit::Authorize on new actions; require verify_authorized and verify_policy_scoped in ApplicationController.
antipattern.reason: Pundit does not enforce authorization automatically without these hooks.

antipattern.description: Generated migrations are sometimes destructive (drop_column without backfill). Require reversible migrations and review.
antipattern.reason: Irreversible migrations cannot be rolled back during deploys.

antipattern.description: Agents may set config.active_job.queue_adapter = :inline for "tests", masking timing bugs in production where Sidekiq is async.
antipattern.reason: Inline mode hides concurrency issues that emerge with async workers.
